rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.

rules_evaluated.fix_hint: File is a demo script, not an article rough-cut.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No paraphrased techniques or frameworks requiring attribution.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 11:Ask what makes this different from a good chat answer; 15:Let it name the root CLAUDE.md; 27:Ask Claude Code to explain the plan; 35:Ask Claude Code to grep for the old wording; Multiple instances of trainer-instruction language throughout ("Ask Claude Code", "Let it", "Run the customer build", "the room").
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body contains trainer mechanics and implicit trainer subject throughout. File is placed in curriculum/lectures/ (student-facing), but prose reads as trainer script. Either: (a) move entire file below maintainer cut if trainer-only, or (b) rewrite body to address students about what they'll observe, not trainer actions.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Foundational principle: students come in as equals, not pupils.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body contains trainer/facilitator behavior throughout: 11:"Start from the page the room will actually use"; 15:"Let it name the root"; 27:"Ask Claude Code to explain"; 31:"Run the customer build command for acme"; 35:"Ask Claude Code to grep". Per check_student_facing.md §1, "Body prose MAY NOT narrate teacher / trainer / facilitator behavior, demo setup, timing, or room-management instructions."
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: File placement in curriculum/lectures/ indicates student-facing content, but body is written as trainer delivery script. Move procedural content below <!-- maintainer --> or clarify file's actual audience (trainer-only → different directory structure).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
